title: World Cup 2026 : Ivory coast squad officially unveiled
original_text:
Emerse Fae officially unveiled the Ivory coast squad for the World Cup 2026 during a presentation broadcast on RTI1 this Friday. The Ivorian coach selected several experienced players, including Franck Kessié, Evan Ndicka, Nicolas Pépé and Wilfried Singo, while Simon Adingra earned his place after an impressive run of form with AS Monaco.

The coach also decided to include Ange-Yoan Bonny, who could experience his first major tournament with the national team during this World Cup campaign.

The Ivory coast squad for the World Cup 2026 combines experience, physical strength and attacking pace as the Elephants prepare to compete against the world’s biggest nations. Sébastien Haller did not secure a place in the main squad, but he still appears on the reserve list ahead of the competition. Ivory Coast will count on players such as Amad Diallo, Evann Guessand and Elye Wahi to lead the attack and help the country chase a strong run at the tournament.

Goalkeepers : Yahia Fofana, Mohamed Koné, Alban Lafont.

Defenders : Emmanuel Agbadou, Clément Akpa, Ousmane Diomandé, Guéla Doué, Ghislain Konan, Odilon Kossounou, Evan Ndicka, Wilfried Singo.

Midfielders : Seko Fofana, Parfait Guiagon, Christ Inao Oulaï, Franck Kessié, Ibrahim Sangaré, Jean-Mickaël Seri.

Forwards : Simon Adingra, Ange-Yoan Bonny, Amad Diallo, Oumar Diakité, Yan Diomandé, Evann Guessand, Nicolas Pépé, Bazoumana Touré, Elye Wahi.

Reserves : Ira Tapé, Christopher Operi, Malick Yalcouyé, Martial Godo, Sébastien Haller.
_note: 全文走 summary 块A(LLM三判后只增不改);processed=成熟仓库累积,raw=本次收集过程。
